student_message: I guess so. But I forgot what symmetry means.
final_message:
Math can definitely be hard sometimes, so please don't feel sad! Forgetting words is just a normal part of learning. You just noticed that the wings match, which is a fantastic observation, and you are doing great! 🌟 

Symmetry just means that if you fold a shape in half, both sides are perfect mirror images of each other. That fold is called a line of symmetry. Because the left wing perfectly matches the right wing, it has symmetry! You can even use this idea to make shapes. If you draw half of a shape on a folded piece of paper and cut it out, unfolding it gives you the whole perfectly matched shape. Does thinking about folding paper help the mirror idea make a little more sense?

orchestrator_intent.goal: Explain the basic meaning of symmetry in a simple way and encourage the student.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student is sad and forgot the core concept. Dialogue will explain symmetry simply, while encouragement provides emotional support.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
